parsed_review.summary: CP00-601 is a descriptor-only RP19.P07 workflow-permission-slice closeout pack (40 units, RP19.P07.M03.S21-RP19.P07.M05.S16, risk B), reviewed via Read/Grep/Glob only. The production_ready claim is honest: manifest and command-evidence declare production_ready:false, runtime_ready:false, implementation_layer:descriptor, no_real_data:true, writes_product_state:false, and the pack does not assert enterprise trust or Claude final approval. All 7 required public exports exist in service.js/validators.js and re-export through index.js. The deliverable distribution (claude_review2/implementation4/failure_recovery24/security_audit3/hermes_evidence3/fixture2/test2=40) and micro-phase counts (2/22/16) match the baseline and contract cp601_requirements, and are enforced by validateClientPortalCoreCp601Coverage. Every descriptor row carries descriptor_only/runtime_execution:false/customer_safe_errors_only/raw_payload_included:false, freezeCp601Result stamps comprehensive no-write/no-leak/no-real-data attestations, and the product source performs no fs/network/process I/O. The contract projection is deep-equal-verified against generated descriptors by the validator, and tests assert coverage, descriptor-only rows, and authority boundaries (claude_is_final_approval:false, no enterprise trust from local validation). The Hermes evidence packet is deterministic control-plane only (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false; production_ready_candidate reflects descriptor validity, not product readiness), satisfying the no-runtime-embedding boundary. command-evidence records 28 commands at exit 0; readiness auth-preflight passed with valid JSON and no prior invalid review attempts exist. Post-review evidence files (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are absent by design, produced from this review before commit. Unrelated dirty files (progress-control-room, workbook/*, docx, ldip) are correctly classified out-of-scope. No P0-P3 findings; Claude remains a read-only independent reviewer and not the final approval authority.
